title: chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. puberulus
intro: Chrysothamnus viscidiflorus subsp. puberulus, commonly known as gray rabbitbrush, is a subspecies of shrub native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The gray rabbitbrush has been used to aid in the treatment of various health issues, such as colds, fever, and skin conditions, due to its ability to stimulate the immune system and promote wound healing. The plant can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits, and is often used in traditional medicine and folk remedies.
